pdg_node.statement: (s0: float, inventory: float) -> StateModelSpec {gamma: float, k: float, q: float, s: float, sigma: float}
pdg_node.informal_desc: Construct the immutable market-making state object with model parameters and initial market/inventory values.

declaration.name: initializemarketmakerstate
declaration.type_signature: (s0: float, inventory: float) -> StateModelSpec {gamma: float, k: float, q: float, s: float, sigma: float}
declaration.docstring: Construct the immutable market-making state object with model parameters and initial market/inventory values.
declaration.conceptual_summary: InitializeImmutableParameterizedState. Creates a single immutable state record by binding runtime initial conditions to a preconfigured set of model coefficients. The transform maps two scalar inputs into designated state coordinates and preserves all coefficients as persistent fields in a typed container. This establishes a canonical initial condition for downstream iterative or recursive updates.. Applications: Initialize a control-system state vector for a robot with fixed controller gains and initial pose/error terms., Seed a compartmental simulation in computational biology with fixed rate constants and initial population levels., Create an initial hidden-state object in signal processing with preset noise parameters and starting estimate values., Instantiate a finite-element or PDE solver run-state with fixed material constants and initial field amplitudes.

pdg_node.statement: (state_model: StateModelSpec {gamma: float, k: float, q: float, s: float, sigma: float}) -> QuotePair {bid: float, ask: float}
pdg_node.informal_desc: Deterministically compute market quotes from the current immutable state without mutating persistent values.

declaration.name: computeinventoryadjustedquotes
declaration.type_signature: (state_model: StateModelSpec {gamma: float, k: float, q: float, s: float, sigma: float}) -> QuotePair {bid: float, ask: float}
declaration.docstring: Deterministically compute market quotes from the current immutable state without mutating persistent values.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Deterministic State-to-Ordered-Boundary Projection. Transforms an immutable 5-scalar state vector into a 2-scalar ordered boundary pair using a closed-form arithmetic mapping. The mapping computes a central reference value from baseline and signed imbalance terms, then applies a width/offset term driven by dispersion and sensitivity parameters to produce lower and upper bounds. No persistent state is modified, so identical inputs always yield identical outputs.. Applications: Autonomous control: compute lower/upper actuator command limits from current system offset, gain parameters, and noise scale., Clinical decision support: derive asymmetric intervention thresholds around a physiological baseline using uncertainty and patient-state imbalance., Distributed systems: generate dynamic lower/upper throttling bounds from queue imbalance, latency variance, and tuning coefficients., Materials process engineering: set real-time acceptable process-window bounds from measured drift, volatility, and risk tolerance parameters.
